expectations.text: Spec contains structured behavioral scenarios (precondition / input / expected behavior / postcondition / error cases)
expectations.evidence: Contains 6 structured scenarios, each with precondition / input / expected behavior / postcondition / error cases

expectations.text: Spec covers error paths (e.g., fallback strategy when the summarization LLM call fails)
expectations.evidence: Scenario 2 explicitly describes fallback strategies for LLM call failure and timeout
Scenario 2 includes a full before/after comparison of the message sequence compression

expectations.text: Spec researches existing code and references real module/function names
expectations.evidence: References real function names such as StoreHistoryMessages and GetHistoryMessages

expectations.text: Spec marks uncertain open questions with [TBD] tags
Spec does not write any files to the project source directory
expectations.evidence: Output saved only to the workspace outputs directory

expectations.text: Spec is behavior-oriented, not implementation-oriented (describes what, not how)
expectations.evidence: Behavioral contracts focus on scenarios and expected behavior, with no pseudocode or implementation details
